summary: Joel Spolsky announces that his second book is heading to the printer. The book is primarily a collection of reprinted articles from his blog over the previous four years. He humorously lists three advantages of the physical book format over reading online: professional editing, not having to read on a computer, and the ability to physically throw it at coworkers to make a point.
key_points: Spolsky's second book compiles four years of blog articles into printed form
Professional editing improved grammar and style issues present in the original online versions
Physical books retain persuasive advantages over URLs, including the ability to be hurled at someone
money_quotes: You can hurl it at your boss or coworkers to make a point, and the impact will be much more powerful than emailing a URL.
I have finally learned to use semicolons instead of commas to separate complete sentences, and somebody smarter than me has sorted out all the "whiches" and the "thats."
key_insight: A curated, edited physical book still offers tangible advantages over online content, not least the rhetorical force of being able to throw it at someone.
